title: Priors for the Long Run

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This is a Staff Report (Number 832) by Domenico Giannone, Michele Lenza, and Giorgio E. Primiceri proposing a class of prior distributions that discipline long-run predictions of vector autoregressions (VARs). The priors are conjugate and can be elicited using economic theory to provide guidance on the joint dynamics of macroeconomic time series in the long run. The paper claims substantial improvements in forecasting performance when applied to VARs with standard macroeconomic variables.
key_points: Authors: Domenico Giannone, Michele Lenza, and Giorgio E. Primiceri; published November 2017 as Staff Report Number 832.
Proposes conjugate prior distributions that can be implemented using dummy observations and combined with other popular priors.
Prior distributions can be elicited using economic theory to guide joint dynamics of macroeconomic series in the long run.
Applied to VARs with standard macroeconomic variables; claims substantial improvements in forecasting performance.
JEL classification: C11, C32, C33, E37 (econometrics and forecasting methods).
why_it_matters: This research paper addresses methodological improvements in macroeconomic forecasting using VARs. It is relevant to researchers and modelers who use vector autoregressions for economic prediction, but does not directly address market structure or actionable macro catalysts for trading.
macro_context: The paper contributes to econometric methodology for macroeconomic time series forecasting. The long-run priors based on economic theory could improve forecasts of GDP, inflation, and other macro variables, but the source text does not provide specific empirical results or policy implications.
